content.text: How do I set the default panels for a specific case, user, and institute?

This approach first retrieves necessary objects (institute, case, user) from the store and then gathers the actual panel objects required. Finally, it calls the update method using all collected data.

```python
def update_default_panels(store, current_user, institute_id, case_name, panel_ids):
    """Update default panels for a case."""
    institute_obj, case_obj = institute_and_case(store, institute_id, case_name)
    user_obj = store.user(current_user.email)
    link = url_for('cases.case', institute_id=institute_id, case_name=case_name)
    panel_objs = [store.panel(panel_id) for panel_id in panel_ids]
    store.update_default_panels(institute_obj, case_obj, user_obj, link, panel_objs)
```

The function leverages helper methods and list comprehensions to ensure all required entities are properly initialized before updating the system defaults.
What happens if one of the provided `panel_ids` does not correspond to an existing panel?
If an ID is invalid, the list comprehension will likely raise a KeyError or similar error when trying to fetch it from `store.panel()`. You might add validation before building `panel_objs`.
That makes sense; I should probably handle those missing IDs in my input layer.
